zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Frampton於1375年出生在英國多瑟特其可勒爾時，她的父親Sir Walter de Frampton46歲，母親Margaret le Blount56歲。她於大約1403年與Sir Thomas Stowell在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1436年5月26日在英國薩默塞特可瑟爾斯同享年61歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特可瑟爾斯同聖 多馬 本 康特波也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Frampton于1375年出生在英国多瑟特其可勒爾时，她的父亲Sir Walter de Frampton46岁，母亲Margaret le Blount56岁。她于大约1403年与Sir Thomas Stowell在英国薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1436年5月26日在英国薩默塞特可瑟爾斯同享年61岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特可瑟爾斯同聖 多馬 本 康特波也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Frampton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places so called, of which there are several in Gloucestershire, one in Dorset and one in Lincolnshire. Most take the name from the Frome river in south Gloucestershire (which is probably from a British word meaning ‘fair, brisk’) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. One near Tewkesbury was originally named in Old English as Frēolingtūn ‘settlement associated with Frēola’, a short form of any of various compound personal names with the first element frēo ‘free’. Frampton in Lincolnshire probably gets its name from an Old English byname Frameca (a derivative of fram ‘valiant’) + tūn.

variants.name: Frome
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places so called, such as Frome (Somerset), Frome Saint Quintin, Frome Vauchurch, Frome Whitfield in Stinsford, the lost Frome Billet in West Stafford (all Dorset), Bishops Frome, Canon Frome, Castle Frome, and Prior's Frome and Larport (formerly Frome) both in Mordiford (all Herefordshire). Or a topographic name from the rivers on which these places are located, or simply a name for someone living beside a river of this name, which is probably cognate with Welsh ffraw ‘fair, fine, brisk’. Compare Frampton .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Frampton was born in 1375, in Chickerell, Dorset, England, her father, Sir Walter de Frampton, was 46 and her mother, Margaret le Blount, was 56. She married Sir Thomas Stowell about 1403, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 26 May 1436, in Cothelstone, Somerset, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in St Thomas Of Canterbury's Church, Cothelstone, Somerset, England.

Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Stowell environ 1403, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 26 mai 1436, à Cothelstone, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à St Thomas Of Canterbury's Church, Cothelstone, Somerset, Angleterre.

ko.St Thomas Of Canterbury's Church, Cothel: 영국 서머세트 컷헬스톤 스트 톰아스 프 캉르버이'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Joan Frampton(이)가 1375년 영국 도르세트 치커에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Walter de Frampton(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret le Blount(은)는 56세였다. 그녀는 대략 1403년 영국 서머세트에서 Sir Thomas Stowell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1436년 5월 26일 영국 서머세트 컷헬스톤에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 컷헬스톤 스트 톰아스 프 캉르버이'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Frampton nasceu em 1375, em Chickerell, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Walter de Frampton, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Margaret le Blount, tinha 56 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Stowell aproximadamente 1403, em Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 26 de maio de 1436, em Cothelstone, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em St Thomas Of Canterbury's Church, Cothelstone, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.St Thomas Of Canterbury's Church, Cothel: ст томас оф кантирбури'с чурч, котелстоун, сомирсет, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Frampton родилась в 1375 г. в местности шиккирэлл, дорсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Walter de Frampton было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret le Blount, 56. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Stowell около 1403 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 26 мая 1436 г. в местности котелстоун, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности ст томас оф кантирбури'с чурч, котелстоун, сомирсет, Англия.
